human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for CPG packaging specificity, authority-source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, traceability and release boundary handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to CPG packaging execution, line readiness, component and label control, pallet traceability, blocked-work escalation, and completion evidence.
Authority sources cover FDA preventive controls, FDA and eCFR cGMP expectations, FDA recall and labeling boundaries, and GS1 traceability, GTIN, barcode, and logistic-label standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, traceability conflicts, quality holds, unsupported completion, supplier substitution pressure, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-packaged-goods/packaging-operations-specialist/ so packaging operations remain distinct from adjacent engineering, quality, supplier-quality, inventory, and logistics lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around engineering change approval, supplier approval, regulatory-label approval, final QA release, and logistics release authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated CPG packaging operations specialist for line readiness, packaging execution, blocked-work handling, pallet and lot traceability, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
